item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents consistently reject society-over-individual as a general rule from the first turn through the final turn. Both acknowledge narrow exceptions for emergencies, public health, safety, or disasters, but frame those as limited cases bounded by individual rights. Agent A and Agent B engage the same abuse-of-public-interest and minority-protection concerns, with no visible flip or directional drift.
